description: Make goal source the compiler's only source: relocate the goal-written compiler into internal/, port the remaining Go tooling to goal, and retire the hand-written Go transpiler behind a committed-generated bootstrap.

userStories.title: Record adopted flip decisions
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the flip's layout and trust model written down so the loop and reviewers share one set of decisions before any code moves.
acceptanceCriteria: DECISIONS.md contains a new entry naming the adopted layout: colocated '<file>.goal' (canonical source) + committed generated '<file>.go' per package directory.
DECISIONS.md entry names the adopted bootstrap as committed-generated Go (B-commit), with the corpus behavioral tier as the primary correctness gate once the reference Go is gone.

DECISIONS.md entry states 'self-hosted' means the shipped goal+goalc library closure, and that test/dev infra (corpus, byexample, internal/selfhost harness, cmd/corpus-gen, cmd/build-playground) stays Go.
SELF-HOST-RESEARCH.md is annotated where its 'coexist as peers' / 'tooling out of scope' language (§1, §5) is superseded by this flip.
userStories.notes: Pure decision/record story. The plan (SELF-HOST-FLIP-PLAN.md) recommended split-dir; this PRD deliberately adopts the COLOCATED variant because source and generated share an import path with no module gymnastics and the goal front-end reads .goal while go build reads .go.

userStories.title: Relocate goal compiler source into internal
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the goal-written compiler under internal/compiler so it lives in the internal namespace and bootstraps from its new home.
acceptanceCriteria: Every selfhost/<pkg>/*.goal is moved to internal/compiler/<pkg>/*.goal for token, lexer, ast, parser, sema, project, pipeline, backend, typecheck (and main.goal).
No source file contains the import path substring 'goal/selfhost/'; goal source self-imports use 'goal/internal/compiler/'.
Taskfile.yml bootstrap and fixpoint targets reference ./internal/compiler instead of ./selfhost.
The ./selfhost directory no longer exists.
userStories.notes: Mechanical move + import rewrite. Verified by `task fixpoint` (universal gate) staying green from the new path.

userStories.title: Add task generate for committed Go
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need a generate step that transpiles the goal source to committed Go colocated beside it, so the toolchain can build from goal source.
acceptanceCriteria: `task generate` transpiles every internal/compiler/<pkg>/*.goal to a colocated internal/compiler/<pkg>/<file>.go.
Generated .go files for all nine compiler packages exist and are committed (not gitignored).
`go build ./internal/compiler/...` succeeds against the generated .go.
Generated files carry a 'Code generated ... DO NOT EDIT.' header line.
userStories.notes: Generated .go resolves at the same import path as the .goal source (goal/internal/compiler/<pkg>) within module goal, so no separate module/-C trick is needed.

userStories.title: Add generated-Go drift gate
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need a drift gate so committed generated Go can never diverge from the goal source.
acceptanceCriteria: `task verify-generated` regenerates to a temp location and diffs against the committed internal/compiler tree, exiting non-zero on any difference.
`task verify-generated` exits 0 on a freshly generated, clean tree.
`task check` invokes `task verify-generated` so the drift gate runs in the universal gate for all later stories.
userStories.notes: Folding verify-generated into task check (rather than verifyCommands) keeps US-001..003 from referencing a command that did not yet exist.

userStories.title: Prove goal-built compiler via corpus
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need a compiler binary built from the generated Go to pass the behavioral corpus, proving the goal-sourced compiler is correct end to end.
acceptanceCriteria: A cmd entrypoint (e.g. cmd/goal-gen) builds from goal/internal/compiler/{backend,pipeline,project} via `go build`.
A Taskfile target runs the corpus behavioral tier using the goal-built binary as the compiler.
The corpus behavioral tier passes when compiled by the goal-built binary.
userStories.notes: End of Phase 1: two compilers (legacy Go + goal-sourced) coexist and agree. Fully reversible up to here; nothing legacy deleted yet.

userStories.title: Port goalfmt to goal
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the formatter written in goal so it is part of the self-hosted toolchain.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/compiler/goalfmt/*.goal exists and `task generate` emits colocated .go that builds.
Formatting a fixture set with the goal-built goalfmt is byte-identical to the legacy Go goalfmt output.
Re-formatting already-formatted goal source with the goal-built goalfmt is idempotent.
userStories.notes: Smallest tooling package (143 LOC); plain-Go-superset port first, no idiom rewrite. Start of Phase 2.

userStories.title: Port textedit to goal
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the edit-primitives package in goal because the fixer depends on it.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/compiler/textedit/*.goal exists and generates colocated .go that builds.
The textedit behavior matches the legacy package on a fixture set of edit applications (same resulting text for the same edits).
userStories.notes: Leaf helper (168 LOC), unblocks fix.

userStories.title: Port cap to goal
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the capabilities package in goal because the interpreter depends on it.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/compiler/cap/*.goal exists and generates colocated .go that builds.
Capability allow/deny decisions from the goal-built cap match the legacy package on a fixture set of capability checks.
userStories.notes: Leaf helper (110 LOC), unblocks interp.

userStories.title: Port guide to goal
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the guide surface in goal so it is part of the self-hosted toolchain.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/compiler/guide/*.goal exists and generates colocated .go that builds.
Guide output from the goal-built guide matches the legacy Go guide on a fixture set.
userStories.notes: Small (400 LOC), low coupling.

userStories.title: Port fix to goal
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the autofixer in goal so it is part of the self-hosted toolchain.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/compiler/fix/*.goal exists (covering fix, propagate, resultsig, match, callsite) and generates colocated .go that builds.
The goal-built fix produces byte-identical output to the legacy Go fix across the existing autofix golden fixtures.
userStories.notes: 1060 LOC, depends on textedit (US-007). Autofix golden tests are the parity oracle.

userStories.title: Port interp values and environment
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the interpreter's value model and environment in goal as the foundation of the interp port.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/compiler/interp/ contains goal ports of value.go, env.go, host.go, assert.go and generates colocated .go that builds.
Value construction, equality, and environment scope/lookup from the goal-built code match the legacy package on a fixture set of value/env operations.
userStories.notes: interp is 3722 LOC, split across three stories (US-011/012/013). This is the ~660 LOC foundation.

userStories.title: Port interp evaluator
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the interpreter's expression/statement evaluator in goal so goscript programs execute under the goal-built interp.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/compiler/interp/eval is ported to goal and generates colocated .go that builds against US-011's value/env.
A subset of the goscript behavioral conformance suite that exercises evaluation passes against the goal-built interp.
userStories.notes: eval.go is 1177 LOC, the core of interp. Depends on US-011.

userStories.title: Port interp driver and doctest
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the interpreter driver, derivation, and doctest runner in goal so the full goscript/doctest surface is self-hosted.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/compiler/interp ports interp.go, derive.go, doctest.go to goal and generates colocated .go that builds.
The full goscript and doctest behavioral conformance suites pass against the goal-built interp.
userStories.notes: ~1836 LOC. Completes the interp port. Depends on US-011, US-012.

userStories.title: Spike lsp concurrency in goal
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need proof that goal can express the concurrency the LSP needs (sync.Mutex, goroutines, JSON-RPC over stdio) before committing to the lsp port.
acceptanceCriteria: A probe goal program using sync.Mutex and a goroutine transpiles and runs correctly when built by the goal toolchain.
Findings are recorded in DECISIONS.md: either goal already supports the needed concurrency, or the specific missing compiler capability is named as a blocking prerequisite.
userStories.notes: The goal backend already EMITS chan/go constructs, but the goal SOURCE has never used concurrency. De-risk before the 2292 LOC lsp port. If a capability gap is found, that becomes its own story before US-015. OUTCOME: no gap — probe at features/_spikes/lsp-concurrency (8 goroutines x 1000 mutex-guarded increments) runs through `goal run` and prints 8000 deterministically; emitted Go is a verbatim go/sync.Mutex lowering. DECISIONS.md 'US-014' records it; US-015 may proceed.

userStories.title: Port lsp protocol and transport
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the LSP wire protocol and JSON-RPC transport in goal as the base of the lsp port.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/compiler/lsp ports protocol.go, jsonrpc.go, uri.go to goal and generates colocated .go that builds.
JSON-RPC message framing round-trips (encode then decode) identically to the legacy package on a fixture set of messages.
userStories.notes: ~425 LOC. Depends on US-014 outcome. DONE: ported the protocol/jsonrpc/uri SUBSET (the only files matching AC-1's named set) verbatim to internal/compiler/lsp/*.goal; `task generate` emits colocated .go that builds. Leaf package (foreign stdlib only: bufio/encoding/json/fmt/io/strconv/strings/sync/net/url/path/filepath), no in-module deps. No goal reserved-word collisions (only 'enum' in a string literal, 'match' in a comment). TestPortedLspPackage (internal/selfhost/port_test.go) runs the legacy white-box jsonrpc_test.go (TestFramingRoundTrip = AC-2 round-trip oracle + TestReadMessageMissingLength) and uri_test.go against the transpiled package via BuildTranspiled+BuildAndTest. `task check` + `task fixpoint` green. The other legacy lsp files (server/symbols/codeaction/hover/definition/references/diagnostics/semantictokens) are US-016/017 and are NOT yet ported.

userStories.title: Port lsp server lifecycle
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the LSP server loop and lifecycle handlers in goal so the server initializes and shuts down under the goal-built lsp.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/compiler/lsp ports server.go, symbols.go, codeaction.go to goal and generates colocated .go that builds.
The initialize/initialized/shutdown handshake from the goal-built server matches the legacy server responses on a scripted session.
userStories.notes: ~499 LOC. Depends on US-015. DONE: ported server.go/symbols.go/codeaction.go verbatim to internal/compiler/lsp/*.goal; `task generate` emits colocated .go that builds. server.goal's dispatch references US-017 handlers (definition/hover/references/rename/semanticTokens/compile/publish/openFilesInDir + openFile type) — these are inert forward-dep stubs in us017_stubs.goal so `go build` succeeds (AC-1); US-017 DELETES that file when it lands the real handlers. symbols.goal hit the ast.FuncMod enum-drift (ported ast lowers FuncMod to a sealed interface) so the legacy `switch decl.Mod { case ast.FuncFrom }` became a type switch over ast.FuncMod_FuncFrom/_FuncDerive. The package gained sema/project/fix imports (no longer a leaf): TestPortedLspPackage's gate widened to the full closure (token,lexer,ast,parser,sema,project,textedit,fix) via new lspClosure helper. AC-2 oracle: new internal/lsp/server_handshake_test.go scripts initialize/initialized/shutdown/exit and pins capability advertisement + null shutdown reply; TestPortedLspServer feeds it to the goal-built package (byte-identical to legacy under `task check`). The didOpen->publishDiagnostics path is US-017 and NOT gated here. `task check` + `task fixpoint` green. Commit 323c3d4.

userStories.title: Port lsp query features
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the LSP language features in goal so the self-hosted LSP is feature-complete.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/compiler/lsp ports hover, definition, references, diagnostics, semantictokens to goal and generates colocated .go that builds.
The existing lsp feature tests pass against the goal-built lsp (parity with the legacy responses on the test fixtures).
userStories.notes: ~1422 LOC. Completes the lsp port and Phase 2. Depends on US-016. DONE: ported hover/definition/references(+rename)/diagnostics/semantictokens verbatim to internal/compiler/lsp/*.goal and DELETED the US-016 us017_stubs.goal bridge (the five files now supply openFile/compile/publish/openFilesInDir/definition/hover/references/rename/semanticTokens). Two drift fixes: diagnostics.goal's `d.Severity == sema.Warning` -> comma-ok assertion `d.Severity.(sema.Severity_Warning)` (ported sema models Severity as a sealed interface); goal reserved word `enum` renamed (refVariant param -> enumExpr in 3 files; symKey field + 3 literals -> enumName in references.goal). rangeOf reused from symbols.goal (US-016), not redefined. AC-2 oracle: internal/lsp/features_parity_test.go (7 tests, self-contained, assertions derived from the fixture, NO sema consts / NO symKey field) passes against the legacy package under `task check` AND the goal-built package via new TestPortedLspFeatures (internal/selfhost/port_test.go, reuses lspClosure). The tightly-coupled legacy feature *_test.go web was NOT fed verbatim (diagnostics_test uses sema.Error/Warning; references_test reads symKey.enum; server_test depends on diagnostics_test) — same precedent as US-016's fresh handshake oracle. `task check` + `task fixpoint` green. The lsp port is now complete; US-018 repoints cmd/* and US-019 deletes legacy internal/lsp.

userStories.title: Repoint CLI imports to goal compiler
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need cmd/goal and cmd/goalc to import the goal-sourced compiler so the shipped binaries run on goal source.
acceptanceCriteria: Every cmd/* import of goal/internal/<pkg> for a ported package is repointed to goal/internal/compiler/<pkg>.
`go build ./cmd/...` succeeds and `go test ./cmd/...` passes.
The goal and goalc binaries still produce identical output to the pre-repoint binaries on a corpus smoke set.
userStories.notes: Start of Phase 3 (the flip). Repoint before deletion so the switch is isolated and reviewable.

userStories.title: Delete legacy Go transpiler
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the hand-written Go transpiler removed so goal source is the only source of the toolchain.
acceptanceCriteria: The legacy hand-written internal/<pkg> directories for token, lexer, ast, parser, sema, project, pipeline, backend, typecheck, goalfmt, textedit, cap, guide, fix, interp, lsp are removed.
No remaining .go file outside internal/compiler implements transpiler logic for those packages (only cmd wrappers and Go-only test/dev infra remain).
`go build ./...`, full `go test ./...`, the corpus behavioral tier, and `task fixpoint` are all green.
userStories.notes: Irreversible point. Test/dev infra that stays Go: corpus, byexample, internal/selfhost harness, cmd/corpus-gen, cmd/build-playground.

userStories.title: Promote compiler to final internal layout
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the goal compiler at its final internal/<pkg> paths so the namespace is clean and matches the original goal of the flip.
acceptanceCriteria: Each internal/compiler/<pkg> is moved to internal/<pkg> (now-vacated by US-019), carrying both the .goal source and generated .go.
All import paths and goal source self-imports are updated from goal/internal/compiler/<pkg> to goal/internal/<pkg>; no 'internal/compiler' path remains.
`go build ./...`, full `go test ./...`, and `task fixpoint` are all green.
userStories.notes: Optional-but-intended final namespace promotion. If kept simpler, internal/compiler could remain the home; this story realizes the literal 'selfhost into internal, replacing legacy' end-state.

userStories.title: Update build, CI, and docs
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the Taskfile, CI, and docs to describe the new single-source layout so contributors and automation use it.
acceptanceCriteria: Taskfile.yml generate, verify-generated, bootstrap, and fixpoint targets reference the final internal/ paths.
README.md, REWRITE-ARCHITECTURE.md, and SELF-HOST-RESEARCH.md describe goal source as canonical with committed generated Go, and no longer reference a ./selfhost directory.
`task check`, `task verify-generated`, and `task fixpoint` all pass.
userStories.notes: Phase 4 cleanup.

userStories.title: Remove transition scaffolding
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need temporary flip scaffolding removed so the repo reflects only the final self-hosted state.
acceptanceCriteria: Any temporary entrypoint added for the flip (e.g. cmd/goal-gen) is removed or folded into the shipped cmd/* if it became the real build path.
No reference to a ./selfhost directory remains anywhere in the repo (source, Taskfile, scripts, docs).
`task build`, `task check`, and `task fixpoint` are all green.
userStories.notes: Final tidy. Keep the archived Go reference compiler reachable via git history/tag per US-001's trust note.
